title: it can be understood by the readers that she is a selfless and determined indivi
content:
it can be understood by the readers that she is a selfless and determined individual who is primarily dedicating her life for her family and friends. She don't care about the strain that it may put on her health, but of only what might happen to others if she doesn't make sacrifices on her own. This not only demonstrates how considerate she is of others but also her heroic personality.

-Jennifer

from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 4, 2025 2:42PM
Jennifer Thomas
Hi Vielka,

After reading your input on how Eudora Welty uses symbolism in "A Worn Path" to convey themes of perseverance and sacrifice, I would like to say that I agree with you, especially when you mention that the journey Phoenix Jackson goes through is one of the symbols used to convey the themes of perseverance and sacrifice. During Phoenix's journey to get medicine for her grandson, she goes through all sorts of hardships and challenges. For instance, as she was walking, she came across a bush of thorns on which her clothes got caught onto. For her it was a bit difficult to remove her clothes from the grasp of the thorns while trying not to tear out a part of the clothing. However, in the end she was able to get it out without it getting torn. Due to this problem she had to deal with, these thorns can be perceived to be a obstacle she came across to prevent herself from continuing further into the journey. According to the story, "A Worn Path", Eudora Welty states that her skirts were full and long, so that "before she could pull them free in one place they were caught in another. It was not possible to allow the dress to tear. “I in the thorny bush,” she sai
